questions.prompt: A student begins liking someone in their seminar mostly because they sit nearby every week and have many low-stakes interactions, even though they know little else about them. This is best explained by:
Which scenario best illustrates responsiveness in a romantic relationship?
options: A partner notices distress, responds supportively, and the other person feels understood
A partner gives advice immediately without checking whether it is wanted
A partner stays because there are no alternatives
A partner avoids conflict by changing the topic

questions.prompt: A couple remains together mainly because breaking up would be financially difficult and they share a lease, even though satisfaction is low. This is most directly captured by:
options: Barriers to leaving in social exchange theory

questions.prompt: A person believes that conflict means the relationship was never meant to be and immediately questions the entire relationship after one serious disagreement. This best reflects:
Which response to distress is passive but constructive?
During an argument, one partner says, "You never think about anyone but yourself," rather than describing a specific behaviour. Gottman would classify this as:
In the Strange Situation, an infant who explores, becomes upset when the caregiver leaves, and is soothed when the caregiver returns is showing:
An adult says, "I want closeness, but I find it hard to trust people and worry I will get hurt if I let them in." This most closely matches:
Which criticism of attachment theory was emphasized in lecture?
options: It focuses too much on peer influence in adolescence
It may oversimplify people into categories and make broad universal claims from limited cultural contexts
It only applies to infants and has no adult relevance

questions.prompt: Script theory is especially useful for understanding 2SLGBTQ+ relationships because it highlights:
options: How prescribed roles shape relationships and what happens when people do not fit them
Why these relationships are entirely conflict-free
Why all relationships are biologically determined
Why minority stress only affects individuals outside relationships

questions.prompt: Which finding is most consistent with the lecture on 2SLGBTQ+ relationships?
options: Relationship satisfaction is entirely different from heterosexual couples
Children in these families show inherent developmental problems
Many patterns are broadly similar, while minority stress can create unique challenges
Domestic labour is always divided unequally

questions.prompt: A distal stressor in the Minority Stress Model would be:
Which option best distinguishes prejudice, stereotyping, and discrimination?
options: Prejudice is behaviour, stereotyping is law, discrimination is cognition
Prejudice is attitude, stereotyping is cognition, discrimination is behaviour
Prejudice is implicit only, stereotyping is explicit only, discrimination is structural only
They all describe the same process

questions.prompt: Two student groups become hostile only after they are told there is one prize and only one group can win it. This pattern is best explained by:
A person prefers their randomly assigned team almost immediately, even though no competition exists. This finding most strongly supports:
Which statement best reflects a hierarchy-enhancing legitimizing myth?
options: Groups at the bottom deserve the same outcomes as groups at the top
Social hierarchies are unfair and should be challenged
It is natural and good for some groups to be above others
All inequality results from random chance

questions.prompt: A person insists that poor people remain poor because they must not have worked hard enough. This most directly reflects:
options: Just world beliefs and victim blame

questions.prompt: Which example best demonstrates outgroup homogeneity?
options: Thinking your friend group is diverse but another group is basically all alike
Assuming your own group is morally superior
Feeling anxious in a new social setting

questions.prompt: When awareness of a negative stereotype causes underperformance on a relevant task, this is called:
Implicit attitudes differ from implicit stereotypes because implicit attitudes are primarily:
options: Evaluations, whereas stereotypes link groups with traits
Always accurate, whereas stereotypes are not

questions.prompt: Which criticism of the IAT was discussed in lecture?
options: It cannot be based on response latency
It is best at predicting any one person's behaviour with certainty
It is relative and tends to predict aggregate behaviour better than individual behaviour

questions.prompt: A physician unknowingly spends less time educating patients with larger bodies and provides less preventive care. This is most consistent with:
Which educational finding best fits the lecture on implicit bias?
options: Teacher expectations and stereotypes can shape student outcomes
Implicit bias disappears in schools because grading is objective
Bias only matters in post-secondary settings
Teacher race and beliefs never matter

questions.prompt: The claim that race categories are socially created rather than reflecting fixed natural divisions is central to:
Which example best illustrates intersectionality?
options: Analyzing racism and sexism as fully separate and unrelated experiences
Assuming one identity is always more important than all others
Examining how race and gender interact to shape distinct experiences of discrimination
Comparing only two men from the same social group

questions.prompt: The statement "Women should be cherished and protected by men" is most clearly an example of:
Which pattern best fits the warmth-competence model category described as paternalistic prejudice?
Which example best fits environmental racism?
options: Toxic facilities and environmental hazards disproportionately affecting marginalized communities
General interpersonal prejudice without a policy dimension
Equal exposure to pollution across all neighbourhoods

questions.prompt: Structural discrimination differs from individual prejudice because it involves:
options: Laws, policies, norms, and institutions that systematically disadvantage groups

questions.prompt: A workplace has no rule targeting mothers, but promotion decisions systematically penalize employees who take parental leave. This is best classified as:
Which statement best distinguishes equality from equity?
options: Equality distributes resources based on need, equity gives everyone the same thing
Equality means identical treatment, equity accounts for differing barriers and needs

questions.prompt: According to system justification theory, people from disadvantaged groups may sometimes:
options: Reject all stereotypes about their own group automatically
Show outgroup favoritism or internalize system-justifying beliefs

questions.prompt: Which of Allport's optimal conditions is most clearly present when two groups must combine resources to succeed at the same task?
The Robbers Cave findings are most important for showing that:
options: Competition can create conflict and superordinate goals can reduce it
Social identity is irrelevant when resources are scarce

questions.prompt: Which proposed mechanism helps explain why intergroup contact can reduce prejudice?
A major criticism of contact theory is that it can:
options: Assume prejudice reduction is equally easy in all power contexts and place burden on marginalized groups
